results.title: Data Scientist - Multifamily Revenue Management | Zenvora
highlights:
Data Scientist - Multifamily Revenue Management | Zenvora
...
# Data Scientist - Multifamily Revenue Management
...
We're a profitable, growth-stage company building industry-leading martech and data products for the PropTech space. Our focus is on our category-leading AI and data SaaS products with triple-digit growth rates.
...
ApartmentIQ is the all-in-one data platform powering smarter decisions across multifamily—covering 40M units with daily property and market intelligence used for surveys, research, and revenue management. We're trusted by 65% of the NMHC Top 50. Our sister company, MavenAI, delivers AI-powered marketing automation built by industry experts and integrated directly with property management systems, serving over 2M units.
...
### The Role
...
We are seeking a Data Scientist to bridge the worlds of multifamily revenue management and data science. ApartmentIQ is the market intelligence layer that operators, asset managers, and RM platforms rely on to price millions of apartment units — and this role sits at the center of making that intelligence sharper, more actionable, and more defensible.
...
This is the right role for someone with real hands-on experience in how multifamily pricing decisions get made: the tradeoffs between occupancy and rate, how concessions affect net
...
rent, how renewal
...
new lease strategy
...
behavior. You
...
ll apply that expertise
...
-driven products that help the industry price smarter.
...
You'll work closely with leadership, product, and engineering to design and deliver features that make multifamily pricing intelligence more actionable — and you'll be expected to use AI tools aggressively as a core part of how you work, not as an afterthought.
...
- Apply LLMs and other AI techniques to accelerate research, automate workflows, and unlock new insights from structured and unstructured market data.
...
- Stay current on multifamily industry trends, data sources, AI/ML advances, and pricing methodologies.
...
- Experience applying LLMs or AI/ML techniques to accelerate research, automate analysis, or extract insights from data.
...
- Strong quantitative and analytical skills with proficiency in SQL.
...
- Familiarity with modern data science practices, including model building, experimentation, and validation.
...
- Proficiency in Python or similar.
- Experience building customer-facing analytical products or dashboards.

results.title: Invitation Homes Software Engineer Interview Questions 2026
highlights:
Software Engineer Interview Questions
...
What is a Software Engineer
...
A **Software Engineer** at **Invitation Homes** plays a critical role in building and maintaining the technology platforms that power the nation's premier single-family home leasing company. With a portfolio of tens of thousands of homes, the business relies heavily on sophisticated digital solutions to manage the entire leasing lifecycle. As an engineer here, you will design, develop, and optimize applications that directly impact thousands of residents and field associates daily.
...
The work spans a diverse range of internal and external products, including resident portals, property search engines, automated maintenance dispatch systems, and internal financial tools. The engineering team is tasked with solving complex logistics, scheduling, and data-synchronization challenges. By joining this team, you will help modernize real estate technology, ensuring that property management is as seamless and automated as possible.
...
This position offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of physical real estate and modern cloud technology. Because the company is continually scaling its operations, your contributions will directly influence system reliability, operational efficiency, and the overall customer experience. You will work in a collaborative environment where clean code, scalable architecture, and user-centric design are highly valued.
...
**Technical Bread
...
**, **React
...
**Algorithmic Problem-Solving** – You will face live coding exercises during the technical screens and onsite rounds. Focus your preparation on fundamental data structures (arrays, strings, hash maps, linked lists) and algorithms. The coding challenges typically range from easy to medium difficulty, so speed, accuracy, and clear communication of your thought process are key.
...
As a **Software Engineer** at **Invitation Homes**, your daily work will directly impact the operational efficiency of the business and the living experience of thousands of residents. You will be responsible for translating business requirements into robust, scalable software solutions.
...
with product managers, UX designers
...
In addition to feature development, you will focus on maintaining the health of the codebase. This includes writing automated unit and integration tests, participating in peer code reviews, and identifying areas of the system that require refactoring or performance optimization. You will also play a role in monitoring production systems, ensuring high availability, and quickly resolving any
...
anomalies that arise.
...
### Role Requirements & Qualifications
...
* **Must-have technical skills** – Strong proficiency in **JavaScript** (ES6+), **React**, **Node.js**, and relational databases such as **PostgreSQL**.
...
* **Must-have architectural skills** – Solid understanding of **Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)**, RESTful API design, and modern web application architecture.
...
* **Nice-to-have skills** – Experience with cloud platforms (AWS or Azure), containerization tools like Docker, and automated testing frameworks (Jest, Mocha, or Cypress).
...
\*\*A: \*\* The core stack is centered around modern web technologies, specifically **JavaScript
...
**React**
...
the frontend, **Node.js**
...
for data storage.
...
A **Software Engineer** role at **Invitation Homes** offers a compelling opportunity to build highly impactful software that bridges physical operations and modern cloud technology. By preparing thoroughly across full-stack JavaScript fundamentals, relational database design, and core OOP principles, you can position yourself as a strong candidate for this growing engineering team. Focus on conveying not only your technical expertise but also your collaborative mindset and interest in their business model.

results.title: Data Scientist Job in Miami | Lennar Homes
highlights:
# Data Scientist
...
- We require a bachelors degree in data science, mathematics, statistics, engineering, computer science, or a related discipline, or equivalent hands-on experience. - We expect 3+ years of experience using data science to solve business challenges and deliver actionable insights. - We require 2+ years of SQL development experience. - We value exposure to AI or machine learning solutions in academic or business settings. - We look for an interest in helping us evolve into a more insights-driven organization. - We need the ability to shape data science methods around practical business problems. - We value experience supporting analytics or modeling efforts that improve business performance. - We require proficiency in preparing data for analytics, reporting, and machine learning applications. - We look for experience developing and validating data science models or analytical solutions in applied, academic, or business environments. - We expect working knowledge of machine learning principles, statistics, and model evaluation techniques. - We value experience contributing to data applications, dashboards, or analytics products. - We appreciate familiarity with AI, automation, or emerging analytics technologies. - We value familiarity with Agile practices such as Scrum or Kanban. - We prefer experience with machine learning libraries such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, Keras, or similar tools. - We expect understanding of core software engineering practices, including version control, testing, and code structure. - We value familiarity with cloud platforms, databases, and data infrastructure concepts. - Experience with NoSQL or big data technologies is a plus. - Experience with knowledge graphs or graph-based analytics is a plus. - This role is primarily sedentary and requires the ability to operate computer equipment with finger dexterity. - We may require up to 10% travel to divisions within our organization. - We look for strong interpersonal skills, a solid work ethic, teamwork, openness to constructive feedback, and willingness to cross-train as needed.
...
- We build, test, and refine predictive and analytical models for internal stakeholders using structured and unstructured data. - We iterate on AI development, including agents and pipelines. - We support measurement, monitoring, and analysis to drive continuous improvement. - We prepare, cleanse, and combine data from multiple sources using strong analytical and engineering practices. - We collaborate with cross-functional teams to access data and deepen data understanding. - We partner with engineering teams to help deploy models into business applications. - We take part in experimentation and iterative development to improve models and business outcomes. - We communicate technical results and data science concepts to analysts and non-technical stakeholders. - We contribute to knowledge sharing and support our teammates as needed.
...
We are Lennar, one of the nations leading homebuilders and a Fortune 500 company recognized among the top homebuilders in the United States. Our mission is to create an extraordinary experience for our homeowners, communities, and associates by building quality homes, delivering exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities where we live and work, and fostering opportunity and growth throughout our careers. This Data Scientist II role sits within our Digital Experience organization, where we use data and analytics to improve business outcomes across Lennar. We offer a supportive workplace with comprehensive benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage; a 401(k) plan with company match; paid parental leave; associate assistance support; education assistance; adoption assistance; vacation, holiday, sick, and personal time; referral bonuses; home purchase discounts; and special company events. We are committed to an inclusive, growth-oriented environment and we are an equal opportunity employer.

results.title: Remote Lead Machine Learning Engineer - Boston | Lennar Homes
highlights:
Remote Lead Machine Learning Engineer - Boston | Lennar Homes
...
# Lead Machine Learning Engineer - Boston
...
- Bachelors degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or a closely related technical discipline. - 7+ years of software engineering experience, including ownership of production services or platforms in a cloud environment. - 5+ years of practical MLOps or ML platform experience, deploying, observing, and retraining models in production at scale. - Strong hands-on experience with AWS SageMaker, ideally including SageMaker Unified Studio, model training jobs, endpoints, batch transform, and pipelines. - Deep familiarity with experiment tracking, model registries, and retraining workflows using MLflow, Weights & Biases, or similar tools. - Advanced Python skills with a history of delivering modular, well-tested, production-grade code; infrastructure-as-code experience, preferably with Terraform. - Solid understanding of batch and real-time inference tradeoffs, including latency, throughput, cost, and operational complexity. - Demonstrated ability to work effectively with data scientists, reduce friction in their workflow, and turn modeling needs into dependable platform capabilities. - Comfort working independently in ambiguous settings, setting priorities, estimating timelines realistically, and escalating blockers proactively. - Bonus: experience with feature stores, model gateways, GPU workloads, distributed training, drift monitoring tools, or supporting both classical ML and LLM-based models on one platform.
...
We are Lennar, one of the nations leading homebuilders and a Fortune 500 company, recognized for creating quality homes, delivering exceptional customer experiences, and fostering a culture of opportunity, growth, and community impact. We are hiring a remote Lead ML Engineer to join our Applied AI & Data Science team and help move our machine learning models from notebook to production. This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, ML platforms, and applied data science, with close collaboration across data scientists, AI engineers, and platform teams. We offer the opportunity to make a meaningful impact across more than 40 divisions, end-to-end ownership, a growth-oriented culture, occasional travel for team activities and meetings, and a remote work arrangement with a preference for candidates based in Miami, FL, Bentonville, AR, or Dallas, TX. Our benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k) matching, bonus and commission eligibility, paid parental leave, education assistance, adoption assistance, vacation, holiday, sick, and personal time, plus additional associate perks and home purchase discounts.

results.title: Lead Data Scientist Job in Boston | Lennar Homes
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist Job in Boston
...
Lennar Homes
...
# Lead Data Scientist
...
Lennar Homes
...
- We require a bachelors or masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Math, Computer Science, Data Science, Machine Learning, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. - We need 5+ years of relevant experience as a data scientist, machine learning engineer, or applied AI developer delivering production-ready models and systems; candidates with a PhD need 1+ year, and those with an MS need 3+ years. - We expect strong proficiency in Python and SQL, plus experience owning the full data science stack from data pipelines through model deployment. - We look for hands-on experience with AI development frameworks such as LangChain, Strands, Amazon Bedrock, AgentCore, or similar tools. - We require experience with experimentation methods including A/B testing, uplift modeling, multi-armed bandits, and causal machine learning. - We value exposure to machine vision techniques such as CNNs, transfer learning, and embeddings, as well as NLP methods including embeddings, transformers, and prompt engineering. - We expect familiarity with AI agent observability and evaluation tools such as LangFuse, RAGAS, Weights & Biases, or custom monitoring solutions. - We need experience integrating systems through APIs, A2A protocol, MCP servers, and orchestration pipelines. - We look for comfort working with large-scale, imperfect real-world data and making progress in complex environments. - We require strong engineering capability to design and maintain production pipelines, microservices, and scalable systems. - We value a proven ability to navigate ambiguity, prototype quickly, and move solutions into production. - We need a collaborative communicator who can align technical solutions with business priorities across varied stakeholders. - Bonus: we consider experience with RAG pipelines, LLM fine-tuning, RLHF, multi-agent orchestration, feature stores, survival analysis or churn modeling, and attribution modeling an advantage. - This is primarily a sedentary office role that requires the ability to operate computer equipment and perform physical movements such as speaking, hearing, bending, stooping, reaching, lifting, and carrying up to 25 lbs, with finger dexterity required.
...
We are Lennar, one of the nations leading homebuilders and a Fortune 500 company, focused on creating exceptional experiences for our homeowners, communities, and associates through quality homes, strong customer service, and community involvement. In this Lead Data Scientist role, we give you the opportunity to shape how we price, sell, and personalize customer experiences across more than 40 divisions by building advanced models and AI agents end to end, from research through production monitoring. We offer a supportive environment with comprehensive benefits including medical, dental, and vision coverage; a 401(k) with company match; paid parental leave; education assistance; adoption assistance; vacation, holiday, sick, and personal time; referral bonuses; home purchase discounts; and more. We also foster growth, innovation, and collaboration while complying with equal opportunity and fair employment practices.

results.title: Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host - Careers at Airbnb
highlights:
Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host - Careers at Airbnb
...
# Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host
...
You will join the Guest and Host Data Science organization, which powers products and data-driven decisions over Airbnb’s marketplace of Guest and Hosts. You will join a collaborative team of data scientists, analysts, engineers, product managers and designers who build, launch and iterate on our product roadmap to drive growth and deeper sophistication through experimentation, modeling, foundational tools and insights. Our work is core to ensuring that Airbnb’s strategy roadmap stays highly relevant for our users while supporting the long-term health of our marketplace. We work directly with decision makers in engineering and product to develop data-driven frameworks, technical solutions, evaluate product changes and guide the company through rigorous measurement and modeling.
...
- Build observational causal inference models to deeply understand guest intent, identify underlying mechanisms, and build data products to measure impact, and inform feature objectives and tradeoffs
...
- Champion AI-enabled Product Understanding, to scale data-driven frameworks, robust measurement, and compelling storytelling of data learnings to guide recommendations and roadmaps with senior leaders
...
Marketplace Optimization and Ranking
...
- Develop models and analytic frameworks that improve listing ranking and recommendation. This includes incorporating marketplace dynamics such as listing availability, supply quality, and long-term ecosystem health into ranking decisions.
...
- Partner closely with Machine Learning Engineers and Product to launch high-impact search improvements. Design experiments and evaluation frameworks to understand the impact of search and AI-driven product improvements.
...
- Build scalable data products and modeling pipelines that power ranking features, query understanding, and personalization. Your work will help enable the next generation of AI-powered search experiences at Airbnb
...
- Connect marketplace thinking
...
experimentation across teams and
...
direction for modeling and experimentation in search, ranking and personalization.
...
The ideal candidate will be curious, optimistic, and relentless at leading innovation in using AI tools to scale experimentation and data-driven decisions, development of domain-relevant input metrics, and continuous understanding of tradeoffs and end-to-end guest product journeys. You will hold a high bar for customer experience and a strong desire to accurately understand user behavior and drive business growth over both Homes and non-Homes verticals.
...
Your expertise:
...
- Causal inference expertise. Experience with machine learning techniques in a marketplace product setting
- 9+ years of relevant industry experience and a Master’s degree or PhD in a quantitative field
- Strong coding skills in SQL and either Python or R.
- Comfort with building proof-of-concept prototypes. Passionate about AI and possessing a learner’s attitude.
...
- Proven ability to be driven and succeed in collaborative environments with cross-functional stakeholders and also in independent work settings. Ability to autonomously set a roadmap for business impact.

You own it end-to-end.We don’t hire researchers to write internal reports. We hire them to build things that work.
...
What You’ll Work OnThe problems in this domain are genuinely unsolved. User intent in property search is noisy, multi-signal, and shifts over time. Outcome labeling is ambiguous. Latency constraints are tight. Ground truth is delayed by months.You’ll work across:
...
- Domain-specific model fine-tuning and continuous learning pipelines
- Semantic understanding for search — going well beyond what the user typed
- Behavioral modeling from interaction data
- Evaluation infrastructure that actually tells us when things get better
...
What We Need: The fundamentals. Fine-tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, full and when each is the right call. Dense and sparse retrieval, reranking, and hybrid search. Evaluation design on tasks where ground truth is noisy or delayed. You need to know this work, not just know of it.Speed. We move fast. The gap between “interesting direction” and “working prototype” should be days for you, not months. If you need a lot of process to do good research, this isn’t the right environment.Production instinct. You don’t need to be a software engineer. You do need to be able to get a model serving in a real environment. Notebooks are a starting point, not a finish line.Depth over breadth. We’d rather you know one area of this field exceptionally well than have surface familiarity with all of it. Strong fundamentals travel. Shallow exposure doesn’t.Experience in conversational AI, dialogue systems, or behavioral modeling is a strong signal. Real estate domain knowledge is useful but not a requirement — the domain can be learned quickly with the right foundation.
...
- A research mandate with real scope; this is not a research-flavored engineering position
- Direct access to the founding team from day one
- Equity commensurate with how foundational this role is
- Competitive compensation , we’ll be straightforward about numbers in the first conversation
- A publication roadmap that’s treated as a first-class company commitment
...
How to Apply: Send your CV and two or three sentences on what area of your PhD work you’re most proud of and why. That’s it. We’ll take it from there.We review applications on a rolling basis and respond to every one.No recruiters. No agencies.

results.title: Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host Marketplace AI | Hirevector
highlights:
Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host Marketplace AI | Hirevector
...
# Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host Marketplace AI
...
Guest Experience is responsible for Search and Discovery, Merchandising, and end-to-end online software experience worldwide from top-of-funnel discovery through post-booking engagement. We are looking for a talented Data Scientist to join the Guest Data Science team. This tech lead will be at the core of using AI and casual inference to understand user behavior in our app, detect underlying intent, and to build data products needed to provide a compelling and personalized experience of our app. We build products that help guests find the inventory most relevant to them in our marketplace.
...
As a Senior Staff Data Scientist, you will lead innovation efforts to build methodologies to identify and evaluate the preferences important to every guest, at every step of their online discovery funnel. You will be instrumental in shaping our data strategy to drive guest conversion and regular engagement with the Airbnb platform across our Homes, Experiences and Services business verticals.
...
The ideal candidate will demonstrate to be a domain expert with granular user behavior and sequence datasets in a marketplace setting. You will hold a high bar for our customer experience and balance a passion for AI with a strong desire to accurately understand user behavior and business success. Your efforts towards personalized content discovery will help make Airbnb feel tailored for you, maintaining a dialogue with our guests to deeply understand their preferences and reflect that understanding back to them, enabling every guest to easily discover the travel they will love.
...
- Learn: Develop deep understanding of how guests navigate and re-engage with our app via analysis, research, and by leveraging granular user action and sequence datasets.
...
- Partner: With product and engineering, drive technical frameworks and science leadership to explore innovative paradigms for detecting revealed preferences and quantifying online frictions.
...
- Build: Write code for prototypes to detect and quantify taxonomy of guest preferences via iterative development of data frameworks, models and artefacts derived from AI toolkits
...
- Evaluate: Assess assumptions and efficacy of derived guest preferences via measurement and validating hypotheses linked to online guest action and engagement. Setup experiments and data feedback loops to own a high bar over continuous impact.
...
- Your Expertise:
...
- Causal inference expertise with marketplace experience
- Preferred domain experience in search, UX discovery, personalized evidence systems
- Advanced degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics or related field.
- 9+ years of industry experience with a PhD (or 12+ years with a Masters)
...
- Strong in communication with XFN partners in product, engineering, and design to enable data-driven product development with a focus on the user experience.
...
- Expert in at least one programming language for data analysis (Python or R) with familiarity in SQL. Comfort with developing proof-of-concept prototypes. Passionate about AI and possessing a learner’s mindset towards LLMs and dynamic systems.

- Every new HelloData feature begins with a data strategy. As part of the Data Engineering team the Principal Machine Learning Engineer will sit at the intersection of data science and production engineering. You aren’t just a researcher; you are a builder who knows how to take a sophisticated ML model and weave it into a high-scale, production-ready ecosystem.
...
- This role is for a technical visionary who will partner with our product team and our Principal Product Engineer to design the data systems that power our next generation of features. You will be the data architect on steroids, ensuring that our automated pipelines are robust, our AI outputs are reliable, and our data infrastructure scales ahead of our massive growth.
...

...

...

...

- Our HelloData product solves complex data problems for the multifamily industry, utilizing automated pipelines and AI to provide real-time market insights for the nation's top managers, developers, and investors. Our platform is trusted by the industry’s largest operators to help optimize rents, underwrite operating expenses, and grow NOI with its highly accurate data and user-friendly interface. Since being acquired by Grace Hill in April 2025, HelloData has continued to accelerate at an unbelievable rate, growing ARR by over 300% in 2025 alone and on track for a record-breaking 2026. We combine the agility and innovation of a high-growth startup with the stability and resources of an established enterprise, making us the gold standard in multifamily data analytics.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

You will drive and guide the rest of the engineers to brainstorm, design and develop AI products and features from inception to production. • We're seeking a Senior Staff Engineer who thrives at the intersection of technical depth, architectural thinking, and mentorship. • You’ll collaborate with cross-functional leaders, build resilient systems that operate globally at scale, and help evolve the foundational building blocks behind AI-powered growth systems.

A Typical Day: • Work with large scale structured and unstructured data; explore, experiment, build and continuously improve Machine Learning models and pipelines for Airbnb product, business and operational use cases. • Work collaboratively with cross-functional partners including product managers, operations and data scientists, to identify opportunities for business impact; understand, refine, and prioritize requirements for machine learning, and drive engineering decisions. • Hands-on develop, productionize, and operate ML/AI models and pipelines at scale, including both batch and real-time use cases. • Leverage third-party and in-house Machine Learning tools & infrastructure to develop reusable, highly differentiating and high-performing Machine Learning systems, enable fast model development, low-latency serving and ease of model quality upkeep. • Collaborate actively with engineers to apply ML / AI in their solutions to help validate ideas and guide to the right outcomes. • Partner with ML/AI Engineers in foundations engineering to mentor and develop initiatives that make ML/AI applications a core discipline for non-ML/AI engineers.

We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
...
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
...
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
...
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
...
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
...
- Design and optimize human-in-the-loop Machine Learning (ML) systems for content review, labeling, escalation and continuous model improvement
...
- Build systems to detect emerging content risks and abuse patterns across regions, cohorts and surfaces using statistical, ML and representation-learning approaches
...
- Design intelligent sampling and evaluation strategies to measure rare events, policy recall, false positives/negatives and model blind spots in large-scale content systems
...
- Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning: Build and deploy production AI/ML systems for content integrity and trust content understanding, including feature engineering, model development and evaluation, thresholding, error analysis and end-to-end model lifecycle management. This includes working with NLP and LLM-based models in real production settings.
...
- 5+ years of industry experience in a quantitative analysis role with a Master’s degree in a quantitative field (computer science, statistics etc.), or 2+ years of experience with a Ph.D.
...
- State-of-the-art knowledge of AI/ML models
- Hands-on experience building, evaluating, and deploying NLP and LLM-based solutions, including text classification, information extraction, semantic understanding or generative applications.
...
- Working knowledge of causal inference
...
This position is US - Remote Eligible. The role may include occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager. While the position is Remote Eligible, you must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity. Click here for the up-to-date list of excluded states. This list is continuously evolving, so please check back with us if the state you live in is on the exclusion list.
